title: Customer remote help: screen share + chat preserved (dbg152)
description:
PipeShare support chat customer-initiated remote help now works end-to-end. When a customer requests remote help and the admin accepts, the customer keeps their active chat (no reset to the initial "Need help?" screen), shares their screen via WebRTC video, and the admin sees a live feed in the remote panel while controlling app windows over the data channel.

Fixes include: Accept/Decline prompt pinned above the composer (not in the scrollable message list), no duplicate WebRTC teardown when the admin accept SSE echoes back, poll recovery for customers when SSE is missed, and stale admin-initiated pending remote invites no longer wipe an active chat when the customer requests help.

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers on the OVH private server using PipeShare support chat remote help. Deploy horizon-backend (remote/active query + customer-request cleanup) and client-portal static assets (dbg152). SaaS updates only via releases.html.
changeLog: Remote help Accept/Decline prompt sits above composer, not at top of message scroll.
Customer chat stays active when admin accepts customer-initiated remote help.
Customer shares screen (getDisplayMedia); admin remote panel shows live video feed.
Fix WebRTC stuck at Connecting — skip duplicate admin attach; customer poll token recovery.
Stale admin remote invites no longer reset customer chat during remote help request.
remote/active returns customer-initiated pending sessions for customers.
Cache bust dbg152 (portal runtime + PipeShare manifest).
